headline: AI trade: Investors may want to look outside infrastructure, Nvidia
summary: All three of the major averages (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) ended Friday's session in positive territory — the Dow Jones Industrial Average leading the way and rising 294 points — managing to close out the trading week with gains. This comes after Nvidia (NVDA) reported first quarter earnings results that beat estimates, but ultimately failed to wow Wall Street investors. Morningstar Wealth CIO for the Americas, Philip Straehl, comes on Market Domination Overtime to assess investor sentiment around the AI trade in response to Nvidia's latest earnings print.

headline: Salesforce Grants Equity Awards to Cimulate Under Its Inducement Equity Incentive Plan
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, May 22, 2026--Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the #1 AI CRM, today announced it has granted equity awards under its 2014 Inducement Equity Incentive Plan (the "Plan") to new employees who joined Salesforce in connection with the acquisition of Cimulate. The Plan was adopted by the Salesforce Board of Directors in July 2014, in accordance with New York Stock Exchange Rule 303A.08.

The Plan was adopted by the Salesforce Board of Directors in July 2014, in accordance with New York Stock Exchange Rule 303A.08.

headline: Are mega-IPOs like SpaceX huge opportunities for options trades?
summary: As part of the latest installment of Yahoo Finance's Options Playbook, BayCrest managing director David Boole explains the influence that mega-IPOs, the likes of SpaceX (SPAX.PVT), Anthropic (ANTH.PVT), and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT), are having on markets (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC), possible options trades, and proxy trades on other stocks like Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA).

headline: Salesforce, Other Software Stocks Are Scraping Themselves Off the Floor
summary: Now software stocks are scraping themselves off the floor—but are still behind the curve as big investors stick with what has worked.  Jefferies software analyst Brent Thill calls the rebound a correction from an overblown selloff.  While semi stocks rode the wave of the AI data center investment boom last year, sentiment soured on enterprise software makers like Atlassian,  Workday  and Salesforce.
